question_text: A student heats ice at 0°C. The heating curve shows a horizontal plateau for 5 minutes before the temperature starts rising again. Explain why the temperature stays at 0°C during this plateau, even though heat energy is still being added to the ice.
correct_answer: During melting, the heat energy being added is used to break the bonds between ice particles (latent heat of fusion) rather than increasing the kinetic energy of particles, so temperature doesn't change. Once all the ice has melted, the added heat increases particle movement, causing temperature to rise.
explanation: When a solid melts, heat energy goes into rearranging particles from a fixed structure into a liquid, not into making them move faster. Temperature only increases once melting is complete and all particles are free to move.
